Type_Description: Grade 0: Intact skin, minimal damage; Grade 1: Superficial abrasion; Grade 2: Deep abrasion, severe fx; Grade 3: Crush, compartment syndrome.
Management: Grade 0: Non-op/standard; Grade 1: Standard fixation; Grade 2: Careful ORIF, monitor soft tissue; Grade 3: Staged, fasciotomy, debridement.

Type_Description: Grade I: Small puncture; Grade II: Small contusion, moderate contamination; Grade III: Heavy contamination, extensive damage; Grade IV: Amputation.
Management: Grade I-II: I&D, antibiotics, fixation; Grade III: Emergent I&D, vascular/nerve repair; Grade IV: Replantation eval or amputation care.

Type_Description: Type I: <1cm clean wound; Type II: 1-10cm wound with moderate tissue damage; Type III: >10cm high-energy wound (IIIA: adequate tissue coverage, IIIB: inadequate tissue coverage/needs flap, IIIC: arterial injury requiring repair).
Management: Type I-II: 1st gen Cephalosporin (e.g. Cefazolin); Type III: 1st gen Ceph + Aminoglycoside; Farm/Soil contamination: Add Penicillin; All Types: Tetanus prophylaxis, emergent I&D, and stabilization.

Type_Description: Based on 4 parts (Head, Shaft, Greater Tuberosity, Lesser Tuberosity). Displacement defined as >1cm or >45 deg. Includes 2-part, 3-part, and 4-part fractures.
Management: 1-part: Sling/Physical therapy; 2-part: Closed or open reduction; 3-part: ORIF; 4-part: ORIF or Hemiarthroplasty/Reverse Total Shoulder.

Type_Description: Type I: Anterior radial head dislocation; Type II: Posterior; Type III: Lateral; Type IV: Both radius and ulna fractured.
Management: Adults: ORIF of ulna (radial head usually reduces); Children: Closed reduction.

Type_Description: Even numbers include ulnar styloid fracture. I/II: Extraarticular; III/IV: Radiocarpal joint; V/VI: DRUJ; VII/VIII: Both joints.
Management: Displaced/Instable: ORIF (volar plate) or CRPP.

Type_Description: Type I: <2mm displacement, no block; Type II: >=2mm or mechanical block; Type III: Comminuted, unsalvageable; Type IV: Associated with elbow dislocation.
Management: I: Sling, early ROM; II: ORIF if block/>2mm; III: Radial head arthroplasty; IV: Reduce dislocation + fix/replace radial head.

Type_Description: Type I: Non-displaced (<2mm); Type II: Displaced (>=3mm), stable joint; Type III: Displaced, unstable joint. (Subtypes: A=non-comminuted, B=comminuted).
Management: I: Non-operative (splint); IIA: TBW or plate; IIB: Plate preferred; III: ORIF + ligament recon or arthroplasty.

Type_Description: Type I: Tip avulsion (<=2mm); Type II: <=50% height; Type III: >50% height.
Management: I: Non-op if stable; II: ORIF (suture/screw); III: ORIF mandatory + ligament repair.

Type_Description: Type I: Bending (extra-articular); Type II: Shearing (intra-articular); Type III: Compression (die-punch); Type IV: Avulsion; Type V: Combined/high-energy.
Management: I: Closed reduction + casting; II: ORIF mandatory; III: ORIF with elevation + graft; IV: ORIF + ligament repair; V: Ex-fix + limited ORIF/spanning plate.

Type_Description: Type I: Incomplete/impacted valgus; Type II: Complete non-displaced; Type III: Partially displaced; Type IV: Completely displaced.
Management: I-II: Internal fixation (cannulated screws); III-IV: Arthroplasty (hemi or total) in elderly, ORIF in young.

Type_Description: Type A: Trochanteric; Type B1: Stable stem; Type B2: Loose stem, good bone; Type B3: Loose stem, poor bone; Type C: Distal to stem.
Management: A: ORIF if displaced; B1: ORIF; B2: Revision arthroplasty; B3: Revision with bone graft/special stems; C: ORIF.

Type_Description: Type I: Below fovea; Type II: Above fovea; Type III: + Femoral neck fx; Type IV: + Acetabular fx.
Management: I: Closed reduction or excision; II: ORIF; III: Arthroplasty usually; IV: Address both fractures.

Type_Description: Type I: <30 deg to horizontal (compression); Type II: 30-50 deg; Type III: >50 deg (shear).
Management: I: Cannulated screws; II: Screws +/- derotation; III: Fixed angle device or arthroplasty.

Type_Description: Stable: 2-part, intact posteromedial cortex; Unstable: 3/4-part, comminuted posteromedial cortex or reverse oblique.
Management: Stable: SHS preferred; Unstable: Cephalomedullary IMN preferred.

Type_Description: Type I: Non-displaced; Type II: 2-part; Type III: 3-part; Type IV: >=4 fragments; Type V: + Intertrochanteric.
Management: I: Non-op/IMN; II-IV: IMN (antegrade) +/- cerclage; V: Cephalomedullary nail.

Type_Description: Type I: Lateral plateau wedge; Type II: Lateral wedge/depression; Type III: Pure lateral depression; Type IV: Medial plateau; Type V: Bicondylar; Type VI: Metaphyseal-diaphyseal dissociation.
Management: Type I-III: ORIF if displaced >3mm; Type IV: Often unstable, ORIF; Type V-VI: Complex, dual plating or circular frame.

Type_Description: Type I: <1cm clean wound; Type II: 1-10cm wound with moderate tissue damage; Type III: >10cm high-energy wound (IIIA: adequate tissue coverage, IIIB: inadequate tissue coverage/needs flap, IIIC: arterial injury requiring repair).
Management: Type I-II: 1st gen Cephalosporin (e.g. Cefazolin); Type III: 1st gen Ceph + Aminoglycoside; Farm/Soil contamination: Add Penicillin; All Types: Tetanus prophylaxis, emergent I&D, and stabilization.

Type_Description: Type I: Non-displaced cleavage; Type II: Displaced, no significant comminution; Type III: Comminuted with significant impaction.
Management: I: Early ORIF; II: ORIF; III: Staged mandatory (ex-fix then ORIF).

Type_Description: Type A: Compression (A0-A4); Type B: Distraction (B1-B3); Type C: Translation/Rotation.
Management: A0-A2: Conservative; A3-A4: Optional surgery; B and C: Surgical stabilization.

Type_Description: Type A: Stable (avulsions); Type B: Rotationally unstable, vertically stable (Open book); Type C: Rotationally and vertically unstable.
Management: Type A: Conservative; Type B: External fixation or anterior plating; Type C: Posterior and anterior stabilization.

Type_Description: Elementary patterns: Posterior wall, Posterior column, Anterior wall, Anterior column, Transverse; Associated patterns: T-shaped, Posterior column + Posterior wall, Transverse + Posterior wall, Anterior column + Posterior hemitransverse, Both columns.
Management: Displaced >2mm: ORIF via Kocher-Langenbeck or Ilioinguinal approaches.

Type_Description: LC I-III: Lateral compression (I: sacral buckle, II: crescent fx, III: windswept). APC I-III: Anterior-posterior (I: diastasis <2.5cm, II: >2.5cm post intact, III: complete SI disruption). VS: Vertical shear.
Management: LC I/APC I: Non-op; LC II/APC II: Plating/SI screws; LC III/APC III/VS: Emergent fixation/packing, highly unstable.

Type_Description: Zone I: Alar (lateral to foramina); Zone II: Foraminal; Zone III: Central canal.
Management: Zone I: Non-op or percutaneous; Zone II: Fixation, decompress if displaced; Zone III: Urgent decompression + spinopelvic fixation.

Type_Description: Type I: Pure dislocation/minor wall; Type II: + single large posterior wall; Type III: + comminuted wall; Type IV: + acetabular dome; Type V: + femoral head fx.
Management: I: Urgent closed reduction; II-IV: Closed reduction then ORIF if unstable; V: Depends on Pipkin.

Type_Description: Bennett: Intraarticular 2-part; Rolando: Intraarticular comminuted (3-part); Extraarticular.
Management: Bennett: Closed reduction and percutaneous pinning (CRPP); Rolando: ORIF or external fixation.

Type_Description: Type I: Non-displaced; Type II: Displaced with STJ subluxation; Type III: Displaced with STJ and Ankle subluxation; Type IV: Type III + Talonavicular subluxation.
Management: I: Cast NWB; II-IV: Emergency ORIF to prevent AVN.

Type_Description: Type I: Non-displaced; Type II: 2-fragment articular; Type III: 3-fragment articular; Type IV: >=4 fragments highly comminuted.
Management: I: Non-op; II: ORIF; III: ORIF (young) or arthrodesis (elderly); IV: Primary subtalar arthrodesis.

Type_Description: Type A: Infrasyndesmotic; Type B: Transsyndesmotic; Type C: Suprasyndesmotic.
Management: Type A: Non-op if no medial injury; Type B: Non-op if stable, ORIF if unstable; Type C: ORIF + syndesmotic fixation always.

Type_Description: SAD: Stage I: Lateral malleolus avulsion, Stage II: Vertical medial malleolus fx; SER: Stage I: AITFL sprain, Stage II: Spiral fibula fx, Stage III: PITFL sprain, Stage IV: Transverse medial malleolus fx; PAB: Stage I: Medial malleolus fx, Stage II: Syndesmosis injury, Stage III: Short oblique fibula fx; PER: Stage I: Medial malleolus fx, Stage II: AITFL injury, Stage III: High fibula fx, Stage IV: Posterior malleolus fx.
Management: SAD: ORIF medial (vertical fx); SER: Non-op if stage II stable, ORIF if IV; PAB/PER: ORIF medial + lateral +/- syndesmosis.

Type_Description: Grade 0: No notch; Grade 1: Small notch; Grade 2: Notch condensation (stable); Grade 3: Evolutive notch; Grade 4: First glenoid loosening.
Management: Grades 1-2: Observation; Grades 3-4: Consider revision to reverse total shoulder arthroplasty, structural or nonstructural bone grafting.

Type_Description: Group I: Midshaft (most common); Group II: Distal third; Group III: Proximal third.
Management: I: Sling (surgery if shortened >2cm); II: Often requires surgery (high nonunion); III: Sling.

Type_Description: Type I: Lateral to CC ligaments; Type IIA: Both CC attached to distal fragment; Type IIB: Conoid torn; Type III: Intra-articular; Type IV: Periosteal sleeve; Type V: Comminuted.
Management: I/III: Non-operative; IIA/IIB/V: ORIF (hook plate/CC repair); IV: ORIF.

Type_Description: Stellate (comminuted), Transverse, Vertical, Polar (superior/inferior).
Management: Displaced >2-3mm or extensor lag: Tension band wiring or partial patellectomy.

Type_Description: KD I: Single ligament; KD II: ACL + PCL; KD IIIM: Cruciates + Medial; KD IIIL: Cruciates + Lateral; KD IV: All 4 ligaments; KD V: + Periarticular fx.
Management: KD I: Non-op/single repair; KD II-IV: Multi-ligament reconstruction; KD V: Address fracture then ligaments.
